#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

+program array_indices_array_item
+
+    implicit none
+
+    integer :: arr_idx(2)
+    real ::  arr_1(2, 2), arr_2(2), arr_3(2, 2, 2), arr_4(2, 2)

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

MRE:
```fortran
program test
implicit none

real(4) :: B(3, 3)
integer(4) :: A(3)

B = transpose(B(:, A))

end program
```

GFortran compiles this, but LFortran gives the error:
```
semantic error: `transpose` accepts arrays of rank 2 only, provided an array with rank, 1
 --> test.f90:7:15
  |
7 | B = transpose(B(:, A))
  |               ^^^^^^^

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

#4420 

We convert an `ArrayItem` like `x(loc, loc)`, where `loc` is an array, to:

```fortran
do __1_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 1)
    do __2_t = lbound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2), ubound(__libasr__created__var__0__array_item, 2)
        __libasr__created__var__0__array_item(__1_t, __2_t) = x(loc(__1_t), loc(__2_t))
    end do
end do
```

```fortran
program test
    implicit none

    integer :: a(4), b(4), c(4)

    a = [1,2,3,4]
    b=[1,2,3,4]

    c= a + b
end program
```
This leads to > 8000 lines of final ASR, which I don't particularly understand the need for. The error is a `down_cast` failing an assert where `is_a<T>` fails. MRE:
```fortran
program test
implicit none

real(4) :: B(3, 3)
integer(4) :: A(3)
A = [3,1,2]
B = transpose(B(:, A))

end program
```

GFortran compiles this, but LFortran gives the error:
```
semantic error: `transpose` accepts arrays of rank 2 only, provided an array with rank, 1
 --> test.f90:7:15
  |
7 | B = transpose(B(:, A))
  |               ^^^^^^^


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5139/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5139/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2433027473","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5139#issuecomment-2433027473","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5139","id":2433027473,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6RBQ2R","user":{"login":"advikkabra","id":64316822,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY0MzE2ODIy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64316822?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra","html_url":"https://github.com/advikkabra","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-23T18:01:06Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T18:01:06Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I'll get to that then.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2433027473/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

#4405 

We convert 
```fortran
array_1(:, array_2)
```

to
```fortran
DO i = lbound(array_1, 1), ubound(array_1, 1)
  DO j = lbound(array_1, 2), ubound(array_1, 2)
    array_1(i, j) = a(i, array_2(j))
  END DO
END DO
```

{"action":"opened","number":4902,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/4902","id":2090333664,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc58l_Xg","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/4902","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/4902.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/4902.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4902","number":4902,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Alter type saving logic in CreateLoad","user":{"login":"advikkabra","id":64316822,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjY0MzE2ODIy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64316822?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra","html_url":"https://github.com/advikkabra","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/advikkabra/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Previously, `type_copy` was saved only when it did not equal `type`, but it could be possible that `type_copy` is a pointer and so `type` would be a pointer as well.

Example:
```
